description: A graph-native JSON reference for the full Curiosity OS curriculum. It includes hierarchy, cross-links, retrieval metadata, audit metadata, and stable IDs for future content systems, search, visualization, knowledge management, and curriculum expansion.
design_goals: Preserve compatibility with the original note-style schema through fields like name, universe_category, domain, description, and linked_nodes.
Add graph-native structure through stable node IDs, typed edges, parent/child links, slugs, aliases, and explicit module exposure.
Improve retrieval through search_text, keywords, tags, path_string, indexes, and canonical names.
Keep the graph future-proof for content planning, database import, search systems, visual graph tools, and knowledge operations.

edge_relations.contains: Parent node contains or teaches child node.
edge_relations.part_of: Child node belongs to parent node.
edge_relations.adjacent_in_module: Concepts are neighboring topics within the same module.
edge_relations.related_to: Curated cross-link between semantically related nodes.

coverage_audit.audit_note: All four wings, all five Wing 1 arenas, all thirty-four modules, and all bullet-level curriculum topics from the locked outline are represented either as unique concept nodes or as shared concept nodes attached to multiple modules.
notes_on_shared_concepts: Expected Value is intentionally shared across Wing 1 and Wing 2.
Optionality is intentionally shared across Wing 1 and Wing 2.
Incentives is intentionally shared across Wing 1 and Wing 4.

nodes.name: N001 — Curiosity OS — Master Curriculum Graph
nodes.description: This root node holds the full architecture of Curiosity OS: four wings, five Wing 1 arenas, thirty-four modules, and the atomic concepts that power the whole system. It is designed as both a curriculum map and a reusable knowledge graph. The learner journey moves from seeing reality clearly, to thinking clearly, to making and communicating clearly, to delivering reliably in the real world.
nodes.core_question: How do all four wings fit into one coherent operating system for learning and action?

nodes.name: N002 — Wing 1 — The Reality Hacker’s Toolkit
nodes.description: Wing 1 is the external operating system. It reframes mathematics, logic, uncertainty, and computation as practical tools for reading the world rather than as classroom rituals. The learner studies scale, evidence, systems, causality, incentives, and algorithms so messy reality becomes modelable instead of mystical. Its role inside the overall system is to answer the question: How does reality behave underneath the surface?

nodes.name: N003 — Arena 1 — Number Sense & Scale
nodes.description: This arena develops raw quantitative intuition. It teaches the learner to feel scale, compare quantities, estimate quickly, and check whether a claim even makes sense before reaching for exact arithmetic. This arena belongs to The Reality Hacker’s Toolkit and organizes a cluster of modules that train a common mode of reasoning.

nodes.name: N004 — M01 — Number Sense & Magnitude
nodes.description: M01 — Number Sense & Magnitude is a curriculum module inside Arena 1 — Number Sense & Scale of The Reality Hacker’s Toolkit. Its purpose is to build intuition for size, scale, quantity, and sanity-checking. The module groups together the following core ideas: Orders of Magnitude, Mental Math Fluency, Unit Sense, Dimensional Thinking, Sanity Checks, Approximate Reasoning. Taken together, these topics teach the learner how to reason more accurately in situations involving quantitative reasoning.

nodes.name: N005 — Orders of Magnitude
nodes.description: Orders of Magnitude is an atomic concept inside Curiosity OS. It focuses on thinking in powers of ten so the learner can quickly judge whether something is tiny, human-scale, city-scale, or enormous. It matters because learners make better decisions when they can notice this pattern in real situations instead of only recognizing the term in theory. In practice, the concept supports clearer judgment, more reliable problem solving, and better transfer across domains. It connects especially well to the surrounding concepts in M01 — Number Sense & Magnitude, but it also links outward to related modules across the four-wing system.

nodes.name: N011 — M02 — Ratios, Rates & Growth
nodes.description: M02 — Ratios, Rates & Growth is a curriculum module inside Arena 1 — Number Sense & Scale of The Reality Hacker’s Toolkit. Its purpose is to understand comparison, speed, change, and how things compound. The module groups together the following core ideas: Ratios & Proportions, Rates of Change, Percent Thinking, Linear vs. Exponential Growth, Compounding, Doubling Time. Taken together, these topics teach the learner how to reason more accurately in situations involving quantitative reasoning.

nodes.description: M03 — Estimation & Fermi Thinking is a curriculum module inside Arena 1 — Number Sense & Scale of The Reality Hacker’s Toolkit. Its purpose is to train rough-but-useful reasoning before precise calculation. The module groups together the following core ideas: Back-of-the-Envelope Reasoning, Fermi Estimation, Assumption Stacking, Bounding, Sensitivity to Assumptions, Real-World Approximation. Taken together, these topics teach the learner how to reason more accurately in situations involving quantitative reasoning.

nodes.purpose: Train rough-but-useful reasoning before precise calculation.

nodes.name: N025 — Arena 2 — Uncertainty & Evidence
nodes.description: This arena teaches the learner how to think when certainty is unavailable. It builds probabilistic judgment, evidence handling, and numerical skepticism so decisions can improve even when the future stays noisy. This arena belongs to The Reality Hacker’s Toolkit and organizes a cluster of modules that train a common mode of reasoning.

nodes.name: N026 — M04 — Probability & Base Rates
nodes.description: M04 — Probability & Base Rates is a curriculum module inside Arena 2 — Uncertainty & Evidence of The Reality Hacker’s Toolkit. Its purpose is to teach students to reason under uncertainty instead of pretending certainty exists. The module groups together the following core ideas: Randomness, Likelihood, Base Rates, Conditional Probability, Independent vs. Dependent Events, Updating with Evidence. Taken together, these topics teach the learner how to reason more accurately in situations involving uncertainty.

nodes.name: N035 — Risk vs. Reward
nodes.description: Risk vs. Reward is an atomic concept inside Curiosity OS. It focuses on balancing downside exposure against potential upside. It matters because learners make better decisions when they can notice this pattern in real situations instead of only recognizing the term in theory. In practice, the concept supports clearer judgment, more reliable problem solving, and better transfer across domains. It connects especially well to the surrounding concepts in M05 — Expected Value & Asymmetric Bets, but it also links outward to related modules across the four-wing system.

nodes.name: N040 — M06 — Statistics & Data Skepticism
nodes.description: M06 — Statistics & Data Skepticism is a curriculum module inside Arena 2 — Uncertainty & Evidence of The Reality Hacker’s Toolkit. Its purpose is to help students avoid being fooled by numbers, charts, and claims dressed as evidence. The module groups together the following core ideas: Mean, Median & Spread, Sampling, Noise vs. Signal, Misleading Graphs, Correlation Traps, Statistical Humility. Taken together, these topics teach the learner how to reason more accurately in situations involving evidence.
nodes.core_question: How does Statistics & Data Skepticism improve the learner’s ability to reason and act?
nodes.purpose: Help students avoid being fooled by numbers, charts, and claims dressed as evidence.

nodes.name: N047 — Arena 3 — Systems & Structure
nodes.description: This arena trains the learner to see relationships, flows, constraints, and interactions rather than isolated facts. This arena belongs to The Reality Hacker’s Toolkit and organizes a cluster of modules that train a common mode of reasoning.

nodes.name: N055 — M08 — Networks, Graphs & Flow
nodes.description: M08 — Networks, Graphs & Flow is a curriculum module inside Arena 3 — Systems & Structure of The Reality Hacker’s Toolkit. Its purpose is to understand connectivity, influence, bottlenecks, and movement through systems. The module groups together the following core ideas: Nodes & Edges, Network Effects, Bottlenecks, Flow & Congestion, Centrality, Fragility in Connected Systems. Taken together, these topics teach the learner how to reason more accurately in situations involving systems thinking.

nodes.description: M09 — Constraints, Optimization & Trade-offs is a curriculum module inside Arena 3 — Systems & Structure of The Reality Hacker’s Toolkit. Its purpose is to teach that every decision lives inside limits and that improvement always costs something. The module groups together the following core ideas: Scarcity, Constraints, Optimization, Opportunity Cost, Trade-offs, Local vs. Global Optima. nodes.name: N070 — M10 — Logic & Argument Structure
nodes.description: M10 — Logic & Argument Structure is a curriculum module inside Arena 4 — Logic, Causality & Strategy of The Reality Hacker’s Toolkit. Its purpose is to build clean thinking and argument recognition. The module groups together the following core ideas: Premises & Conclusions, Valid vs. Invalid Arguments, Necessary vs. Sufficient Conditions, Contradictions, Logical Consistency, Common Fallacies. Taken together, these topics teach the learner how to reason more accurately in situations involving reasoning.

nodes.description: M11 — Correlation, Causation & Counterfactuals is a curriculum module inside Arena 4 — Logic, Causality & Strategy of The Reality Hacker’s Toolkit. nodes.name: N092 — M13 — Algorithms & Search
nodes.description: M13 — Algorithms & Search is a curriculum module inside Arena 5 — Computation & Abstraction of The Reality Hacker’s Toolkit. Its purpose is to show that many real problems are procedures, not mysteries. The module groups together the following core ideas: Step-by-Step Logic, Search Strategies, Heuristics, Efficiency, Brute Force vs. Elegance, Problem Decomposition. Taken together, these topics teach the learner how to reason more accurately in situations involving computation.

nodes.name: N099 — M14 — Abstraction, Modeling & Simulation
nodes.description: M14 — Abstraction, Modeling & Simulation is a curriculum module inside Arena 5 — Computation & Abstraction of The Reality Hacker’s Toolkit. Its purpose is to teach students to create simplified models of messy reality. The module groups together the following core ideas: Simplification, Variables & Assumptions, Model Usefulness vs. Accuracy, Simulation Thinking, Edge Cases, When Models Fail. Taken together, these topics teach the learner how to reason more accurately in situations involving computation.

nodes.name: N106 — M15 — Code as Executable Logic
nodes.description: M15 — Code as Executable Logic is a curriculum module inside Arena 5 — Computation & Abstraction of The Reality Hacker’s Toolkit. Its purpose is to make logic tangible by turning reasoning into instructions a machine can follow. The module groups together the following core ideas: Instructions & Flow, Conditions, Loops, Variables, Debugging as Thinking, Code as a Laboratory for Reasoning. Taken together, these topics teach the learner how to reason more accurately in situations involving computation.

nodes.name: N113 — Wing 2 — The Cognition Payload
nodes.description: Wing 2 is the internal operating system. It studies how perception filters reality, how beliefs form and update, how bias distorts judgment, and how learning, agency, and writing sharpen thought. Wing 1 models the world; Wing 2 models the mind that is trying to understand that world. Its role inside the overall system is to answer the question: How does the mind behave while trying to understand reality?

nodes.name: N114 — M16 — Perception, Attention & the Map-Territory Problem
nodes.canonical_name: M16 — Perception, Attention & the Map-Territory Problem
nodes.description: M16 — Perception, Attention & the Map-Territory Problem is a curriculum module inside The Cognition Payload. Its purpose is to realize that the mind does not see reality directly; it builds a filtered model of it. The module groups together the following core ideas: Map vs. Territory, Selective Attention, Salience, Framing, Compression, Narrative Filters, Perspective Dependence. Taken together, these topics teach the learner how to reason and act more effectively in situations involving perception.

nodes.name: N122 — M17 — Beliefs, Assumptions & Updating
nodes.description: M17 — Beliefs, Assumptions & Updating is a curriculum module inside The Cognition Payload. Its purpose is to learn how beliefs are built, defended, and corrected. The module groups together the following core ideas: Belief Formation, Hidden Assumptions, First-Principles Thinking, Inversion, Falsification, Bayesian Updating, Outside View. Taken together, these topics teach the learner how to reason and act more effectively in situations involving belief updating.
nodes.core_question: How does Beliefs, Assumptions & Updating improve the learner’s ability to reason and act?

nodes.name: N130 — M18 — Judgment & Decision-Making Under Uncertainty
nodes.description: M18 — Judgment & Decision-Making Under Uncertainty is a curriculum module inside The Cognition Payload. Its purpose is to improve choices when the future is unclear and information is incomplete. The module groups together the following core ideas: Base-Rate Reasoning, Decision Trees, Regret Minimization, Satisficing vs. Optimizing, Reversible vs. Irreversible Decisions. Taken together, these topics teach the learner how to reason and act more effectively in situations involving decision making.

nodes.canonical_name: M19 — Biases, Failure Modes & Cognitive Traps
nodes.description: M19 — Biases, Failure Modes & Cognitive Traps is a curriculum module inside The Cognition Payload. Its purpose is to see the recurring ways the mind goes wrong. The module groups together the following core ideas: Confirmation Bias, Availability Bias, Anchoring, Overconfidence, Motivated Reasoning, Halo Effect, Survivorship Bias. Taken together, these topics teach the learner how to reason and act more effectively in situations involving cognitive biases.
nodes.core_question: How does Biases, Failure Modes & Cognitive Traps improve the learner’s ability to reason and act?
primary_path: M19 — Biases, Failure Modes & Cognitive Traps
nodes.purpose: See the recurring ways the mind goes wrong.

nodes.name: N137 — Confirmation Bias
nodes.description: Confirmation Bias is an atomic concept inside Curiosity OS. It focuses on noticing the tendency to seek and favor information that supports existing beliefs. It matters because learners make better decisions when they can notice this pattern in real situations instead of only recognizing the term in theory. In practice, the concept supports clearer judgment, more reliable problem solving, and better transfer across domains. It connects especially well to the surrounding concepts in M19 — Biases, Failure Modes & Cognitive Traps, but it also links outward to related modules across the four-wing system.

nodes.name: N144 — M20 — Self-Regulation, Identity & Agency
nodes.description: M20 — Self-Regulation, Identity & Agency is a curriculum module inside The Cognition Payload. Its purpose is to build a mind that can act, recover, and direct itself. The module groups together the following core ideas: Identity & Self-Story, Emotion as Signal, Habit Loops, Motivation Architecture, Procrastination Mechanics, Stress Calibration, Internal vs. External Locus of Control. Taken together, these topics teach the learner how to reason and act more effectively in situations involving agency.

nodes.name: N160 — M22 — Thinking in Public: Writing, Research & Truth-Seeking
nodes.canonical_name: M22 — Thinking in Public: Writing, Research & Truth-Seeking
nodes.module_name: Thinking in Public: Writing, Research & Truth-Seeking
nodes.description: M22 — Thinking in Public: Writing, Research & Truth-Seeking is a curriculum module inside The Cognition Payload. Its purpose is to turn thought into clarity, communication, and more honest inquiry. The module groups together the following core ideas: Writing to Think, Clear Explanation, Steelmanning, Source Evaluation, Evidence Hierarchy, Research Workflow, Synthesis, Intellectual Honesty. Taken together, these topics teach the learner how to reason and act more effectively in situations involving truth-seeking.

nodes.name: N170 — M23 — Communication That Creates Clarity
nodes.description: M23 — Communication That Creates Clarity is a curriculum module inside The Signal Forge. Its purpose is to learn to express ideas so other people can understand, trust, and act. The module groups together the following core ideas: Structure Before Style, Explaining Hard Ideas Simply, Story vs. Argument, Persuasion vs. Manipulation, Audience Awareness, Speaking with Precision, Presenting Without Fluff. nodes.name: N196 — M26 — Digital Making & Tool Fluency
nodes.description: M26 — Digital Making & Tool Fluency is a curriculum module inside The Signal Forge. Its purpose is to give students the power to make things in digital form. The module groups together the following core ideas: Documents, Spreadsheets, Slides, Visual Communication, Basic Data Handling, No-Code Tools, Intro Coding Fluency, Tool Selection Mindset. Taken together, these topics teach the learner how to reason and act more effectively in situations involving digital craft.

nodes.name: N214 — M28 — Publishing, Portfolio & Signal
nodes.description: M28 — Publishing, Portfolio & Signal is a curriculum module inside The Signal Forge. Its purpose is to teach students to turn work into visible proof and compounding signal. The module groups together the following core ideas: Shipping Publicly, Portfolio Building, Case Studies, Credibility through Artifacts, Online Reputation, Feedback Loops from Publishing, Compounding Signal over Time, Proof of Work Mindset. Taken together, these topics teach the learner how to reason and act more effectively in situations involving publishing.

nodes.name: N223 — Wing 4 — The Delivery Engine
nodes.description: Wing 4 turns capability into outcomes. It teaches execution, consistency, collaboration, negotiation, career leverage, and life direction so intelligence becomes visible in finished work, trustworthy relationships, and long-term positioning. Its role inside the overall system is to answer the question: How is capability turned into finished work, leverage, and real-world power?

nodes.name: N232 — M30 — Systems for Consistency
